description: Kenya IDF parameters and rainfall data from RDM 2.1. Kenya uses a different IDF approach from Uganda -- rainfall intensity is derived from 24-hour maximum daily rainfall using Equation 6.5, or from KMD IDF curves directly. The TRRL East Africa Flood Model uses depth-duration relationships per Equation 6.11-6.12 with zone-specific index 'n' values from Table 6.16.
approach: Kenya RDM 2.1 does NOT provide station-specific IDF constants (a, c) like Uganda MoWT Vol 2 Tables 4.5/4.6. Instead, Kenya derives IDF from: (1) KMD published IDF curves for specific towns, (2) Rainfall Frequency Atlas of Kenya, or (3) Equation 6.5 using 24hr maximum daily rainfall.

equation_6_5.description: Site-specific intensity from daily 24hr annual maximum recorded precipitation

trrl_depth_duration.description: East African depth-duration equation used in TRRL method

rainfall_zones.description: Table 6.16: Rainfall Time (Tp) for East African 10 Year Storms -- zone-specific parameters

daily_rainfall_maps.figure_6_1: Daily point rainfall of average recurrence interval 2 years (map of East Africa)
daily_rainfall_maps.figure_6_2: Ratio for different rainfall zones in East Africa (10:2 year ratio map)
daily_rainfall_maps.description: Figure 6.1 provides 2-year daily rainfall isohyets. Figure 6.2 provides the 10-year to 2-year rainfall ratio by zone. Zones covering Kenya include: Zone 1A/1B (Central Highlands, ratio ~1:60), Zone 2 (Arid, ratio ~1:89), Zone 3 (South Kenya Coast, ratio ~1:68). These maps are from TRRL Laboratory Reports 623 and 706.

idf_worked_example.description: Appendix A.2: Example worked-out IDF from sample 24hr max annual daily rainfall

regional_flood_frequency.description: Table 5.3: Regional flood frequency curves for Kenya's major river basins. MAF = Mean Annual Flood derived from Q = coefficient * A^exponent, where A = catchment area in km2.
regional_flood_frequency.notes: MAF = Mean Annual Flood, Q = Flood Discharge (m3/s), A = Catchment Area km2

frequency_factor.description: Table 6.11: Hydrological Frequency Factor (Cf) for Different Return Period -- used in Modified Rational Method
